statement: ∀ n : ℕ, Even n ∨ Odd n
title: Every natural is even or odd
Product of consecutive integers is even

metadata.note: differentiator: needs case analysis or a mathlib lemma

statement: ∀ k : ℕ, ∃ n : ℕ, ∀ j : ℕ, 1 ≤ j → j ≤ k → ¬Nat.Prime (n + j)
title: Arbitrarily large prime gaps

metadata.note: elementary witness: n = (k+1)! + 1, then n+j = (k+1)!+j+1 is divisible by j+1 for 1 ≤ j ≤ k. Likely formalizable but non-trivial.
metadata.hint: witness n = Nat.factorial (k + 1) + 1; show (k+1)! + j + 1 ∣ (k+1)! + j + 1 using dvd_add and Nat.factorial_dvd_factorial

statement: ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → ∃ k : ℕ, Function.iterate (fun m => if m % 2 = 0 then m / 2 else 3 * m + 1) k n = 1
title: Collatz Conjecture

metadata.note: genuinely open; the 3n+1 problem. No finite verification suffices.
metadata.difficulty: one of the most famous open problems in elementary number theory
metadata.decomposability_prediction: degenerate_null — no named partial result gives models a structural anchor; expect near-identical restatements across model families

statement: ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → ∃ p : ℕ, n ^ 2 < p ∧ p < (n + 1) ^ 2 ∧ Nat.Prime p
title: Legendre's Conjecture

literature_anchors.name: Bertrand's Postulate
literature_anchors.relevance: gives a prime in (n², 2n²); Legendre requires (n², (n+1)²) — the gap is a factor of ~n in interval width

literature_anchors.name: Baker-Harman-Pintz (2001)
literature_anchors.statement: for sufficiently large x, ∃ p prime in [x, x + x^0.525]
literature_anchors.relevance: gives prime gap O(n^1.05) near n²; Legendre requires O(n); doesn't close the gap but provides a second decomposition trajectory

metadata.note: genuinely open; there is always a prime between consecutive perfect squares
metadata.difficulty: one of Landau's four problems (1912), still unresolved
metadata.decomposability_prediction: structured_null — Bertrand anchor enables multiple distinct decomposition strategies across model families; expect N_real ≥ 2, hardness ≠ null

statement: ∀ N : ℕ, ∃ p : ℕ, N < p ∧ Nat.Prime p ∧ Nat.Prime (p + 2)
title: Twin Prime Conjecture

literature_anchors.name: Chen's Theorem (1973)
literature_anchors.statement: every sufficiently large even integer = prime + semiprime
literature_anchors.relevance: closest result to twin primes; establishes the 6k±1 canonical form and sieve method applicability

literature_anchors.name: Goldston-Pintz-Yıldırım (2005)
literature_anchors.relevance: small gaps exist infinitely often; foundation for Maynard/Zhang bounded gaps

literature_anchors.name: Maynard (2013) / Zhang (2013)
literature_anchors.relevance: bounded prime gaps proven; direct predecessor to twin prime conjecture

metadata.note: genuinely open; confirmed structured_null in first run (Claude isolated {p | Prime p ∧ Prime (p+2)}.Infinite with real unboundedness structure)
metadata.decomposability_prediction: structured_null — richest anchor set of the open tier (Chen/GPY/Maynard progression); expect 3+ distinct decomposition strategies: (1) Chen sieve path: p + p2, arguing why semiprime→twin requires closing a single gap; (2) GPY/Zhang path: bounded gaps proven for gap=246, 'squeezing' argument toward gap=2; (3) k-tuple/Maynard path: dense prime constellations establish frequency; all fail at same step but via different routes. First-run data confirmed N_real ≥ 2 (Claude produced genuine unboundedness structure, not bare restatement). Highest expected N_real of all open-tier problems.

statement: ∀ n : ℕ, 2 < n → Even n → ∃ p q : ℕ, Nat.Prime p ∧ Nat.Prime q ∧ p + q = n
title: Goldbach's Conjecture

literature_anchors.name: Chen's Theorem (1973)
literature_anchors.statement: every sufficiently large even integer = prime + semiprime (p2)
literature_anchors.relevance: closest result to Goldbach; reduces the hard part to distinguishing prime from semiprime

literature_anchors.name: Vinogradov (1937)
literature_anchors.statement: every sufficiently large odd integer is the sum of three primes
literature_anchors.relevance: odd Goldbach proven for large N; even Goldbach remains open

metadata.note: genuinely open; first-run data shows Haiku padding (finite cases) inflates scalar — use artifact-first reporting for open tier
metadata.decomposability_prediction: structured_null — two distinct anchor trajectories; expect 2 decomposition strategies: (1) Chen path: every large even = p + p2 (semiprime); final gap is semiprime→prime (single Goldbach gap); (2) Vinogradov path: odd Goldbach closed for large N via circle method; even case framed as reduction; both paths fail at the same open step but arrive differently. N_real likely 2 (Chen path + Vinogradov path); slightly less rich than twin-primes which has 3 distinct trajectories. Chen's theorem is the canonical partial result — models that anchor here have the strongest gradient.
